title: Jarava vatroensis
intro:
Unfortunately, I couldn't find any information about the plant Jarava vatroensis. However, I can provide information on Jarava, a genus of plants commonly known as Queñua. 

Jarava is a genus of plants in the family Poaceae, commonly known as Queñua, a type of grass or shrub native to the Andean regions of South America. The health benefits of Jarava, particularly Jarava ichu, include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, improve digestion, and provide anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Jarava include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Jarava include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used to treat various health conditions, including diabetes and digestive issues.

The plant Jarava vatroensis is native to the Andes mountain range, which is consistent with the geographical distribution of other species within the same genus and subfamily.
